arguments.file_text:
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageVersion Include="Azure.Identity" Version="1.13.2" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting" Version="1.15.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="10.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a clear picture. Let me identify all packages and their versions across projects:

| Package | Api | Worker | Shared | Tests |
|---------|-----|--------|--------|-------|
| System.Text.Json | 10.0.1 | 8.0.4 | 10.0.1 | 10.0.1 |
| Azure.Identity | 1.13.2 | - | 1.11.4 | - |
| Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting | - | 8.0.1 | - | - |
| OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting | - | 1.15.0 | - | - |


| xunit | - | - | - | 2.9.3 |

I've spotted version conflicts with System.Text.Json and Azure.Identity, so I'll consolidate to the highest versions across the board. My plan is to set up centralized package management by creating a Directory.Packages.props file at the solution root with all dependencies pinned to their highest versions, then strip out the individual version attributes from each project file and enable centralized version management.

    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="10.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

data.content:
Done. Here's what I did:

**Created `Directory.Packages.props`** with centralized versions, resolving conflicts to the highest version:
- `System.Text.Json` → **10.0.1** (was 10.0.1 in Api/Shared/Tests, 8.0.4 in Worker)
- `Azure.Identity` → **1.13.2** (was 1.13.2 in Api, 1.11.4 in Shared)
- `Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting` → 8.0.1
- `OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting` → 1.15.0
- `xunit` → 2.9.3

**Removed `Version` attributes** from all `PackageReference` entries in all 4 `.csproj` files.
